exemplar.title:
How do Zinc Sulfate, Aluminium Sulfate, Lead Nitrate and Lithium Chloride affect the 
activity of phosphatase enzymes in mung bean sprouts (Vigna radiata)?

detailedExemplarData.title:
How do Zinc Sulfate, Aluminium Sulfate, Lead Nitrate and Lithium Chloride affect the 
activity of phosphatase enzymes in mung bean sprouts (Vigna radiata)?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Comprehensive and accurate explanation of enzyme inhibition and phosphatase function situating the study within biomedical relevance
Clear and focused research question that is revisited throughout the investigation
Logical outline of the procedure with rationale for the choice of colourimetric assay and selected inhibitors
weaknesses: Methodology limited by only three replicates and omission of true positive/negative controls beyond âno inhibitorâ
Insufficient justification for the chosen inhibitor concentration (0.1 M)
Uncertainties provided for some volumetric glassware but omitted for pipettes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates an excellent grasp of the biological topic and communicates it effectively, with comprehensive background and clear context (band 5â6). The research question is clearly stated and focused although it spans four inhibitors, stretching scope slightly (band 3â4). The methodology is generally relevant and logical but lacks full justification and statistical robustness (band 3â4). On balance, performance aligns with a high band 5 for Focus and Method.

strengths: Relevant and coherent application of source material with clear links to enzyme kinetics concepts
Accurate depiction of the chemical equation for phosphatase action and strong descriptions of inhibitor mechanisms
Mostly accurate and consistent use of biochemical terminology
weaknesses: Occasional confusion between absorbance and inhibition magnitude
Minor inconsistencies in chemical notation and occasional lapses in precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work applies literature and source material in a mostly relevant way, demonstrating clear knowledge though not critically interpreting sources deeply (band 3â4). Terminology and concepts are generally used accurately and consistently, with only occasional imprecision. Overall this performance merits the upper half of the 3â4 band, corresponding to a score of 4.

strengths: Use of one-way ANOVA and correct interpretation of p-value to reject the null hypothesis
Identification of precipitation as a confounding factor and proposal of a secondary filtration step
Clear ranking of inhibitor effectiveness linked to mechanism, supported by bar graphs with error bars
Presentation of means and standard deviations underpinning inferential tests
weaknesses: No explicit legend or description of error bars on inhibition curves
ANOVA output lacks details of critical F-value and degrees of freedom
Evaluation of alternative explanations and methodological limitations remains surface-level
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student delivers good analysis relevant to the research question, with sound statistical reasoning and critical evaluation of key confounders, placing performance squarely in the 7â9 band. Conclusions are well supported, argumentation is coherent with minor inconsistencies, justifying a score of 8 for Critical Thinking.

strengths: Report follows a clear and logical structure with effective headings and a comprehensive table of contents
Concise and accurate title that immediately establishes the investigationâs focus
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting and duplicated âonlineâ tags throughout the bibliography
Figure captions duplicated or unnumbered, and occasional formatting artefacts (e.g., stray HTML tags)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the overall structure is clear and appropriate, layout and formatting issuesâespecially inconsistent referencing and figure presentationâprevent achievement of the higher band. The work meets the descriptors for the 1â2 band, yielding a score of 2 for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows the expected EE structure: title page, abstract substitute, table of contents, introduction, methodology, results, discussion, conclusion, bibliography. Headings and sub-headings are used effectively, tables and figures are integrated, and appendices are avoided. Sequencing is logical and facilitates reader understanding, meeting the criteria for a "clear and appropriate structure throughout" (band 3-4).

The student includes a title, stated word count and some pagination, and generally applies MLA/APA-style referencing; images are captioned. However, **formatting is uneven**: figure captions are duplicated, some images lack numbering consistency, citation style is occasionally inaccurate, and page numbers/headers are not clearly evidenced. Minor typographical artefacts (HTML tags in table) detract from professional presentation. These shortcomings align with "some layout considerations missing or incorrectly applied," corresponding to the 1-2 band (score 2).

The argument follows a logical flow from background, hypothesis, data presentation, to conclusion, and **generally answers the research question**. Nonetheless, coherence is weakened by digressions (e.g., extensive biomedical context) and by the unresolved confounding effect of precipitates, which undermines the strength of the central claim. Transitions between sections are sometimes abrupt, and evidence is not always critically weighted. These issues fit the descriptor of an "adequate but inconsistent" argument, meriting a mid-band score.

The student clearly states the biological focus on phosphatase inhibition in mung-bean sprouts and links it convincingly to biomedical relevance (drug design, neurodegenerative disease). The introduction provides **coherent background science, definitions and context**, allowing a reader unfamiliar with the field to follow the investigation. Although some detail is tangential and occasionally repetitive, the overall explanation of enzyme inhibition and phosphatase function is **comprehensive and largely accurate**, satisfying the descriptors for the top band.

An extensive bibliography and in-text citations demonstrate **mostly relevant literature use**, and the student attempts to connect sources to enzymology concepts (e.g., inhibitor types, phosphatase roles). Application within the analysis, however, tends to be descriptiveâsources are cited to provide background rather than to critically interpret results or compare findings. Thus the work meets the "mostly relevant application with clear knowledge but partially effective use" descriptor, meriting a mid-band score.

The student processes data through means, standard deviations and ANOVA, showing **adequate quantitative analysis** that links absorbance to inhibitor effectiveness. Yet interpretation stops at surface level; potential confounders (precipitation, seed age) are recognised only after data collection and not rectified analytically. Graphs lack error-bar explanations and no calculation of enzyme activity rates (e.g., units/min) is attempted. These factors limit depth, positioning the analysis at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The student outlines a logical procedure (extraction, colourimetric assay, ANOVA) and lists equipment and variables, showing **some rationale for choosing colourimetry and the selected inhibitors**. Nevertheless, key methodological weaknesses remain: only three replicates, no true negative/positive controls beyond âno inhibitorâ, failure to address precipitation that confounds absorbance, and limited justification of concentration choice. These omissions indicate a methodology that is "generally relevantâ¦with some evidence of selection rationale," fitting the 3-4 band; performance is nearer the lower end of that band.

The discussion correctly ranks inhibitor effectiveness, links findings to inhibitor mechanisms, and uses ANOVA results to reject the null hypothesis. **Some limitations and improvements are acknowledged** (precipitation, freshness of sprouts, pipette air bubbles). However, evaluation of method reliability is mostly qualitative and lacks depth; alternative explanations are mentioned but not fully explored, and no substantive suggestions for additional data or modified designs are developed. This corresponds to an "adequate evaluation with partially supported conclusions" (4-6 band, lower end).

The research question is explicitly stated, focused on comparing four specific inhibitors at a fixed concentration and is revisited in the analysis and conclusion. However, including four different chemicals stretches the scope and introduces multiple variables (precipitation effects, metal ion interactions) that are **only partially controlled or justified**, so the focus is not fully tight. This aligns with the 3-4 band: the question is clear but only partially focused.

The essay employs biological and biochemical terminology (e.g., "non-competitive inhibition", "mixed inhibitor", "phenolphthalein phosphate") **mostly accurately and in appropriate contexts**. Occasional imprecision appearsâfor instance, confusing absorbance with inhibition magnitude and minor errors in chemical notationâbut these do not impede comprehension. This places the work solidly in the 3-4 band for terminology use; a score of 3 reflects some inconsistency.

annotations.comment: The calculation of mean absorbance using a clear formula demonstrates correct application of statistical concepts and supports transparency in data processing.
The formula for standard deviation is accurately presented and contextualized with a concrete example, reinforcing methodological transparency.
The bar graph of final absorbance data integrates error bars effectively, visually confirming reduced variability and enhancing confidence in the reported means.
The rationale for using a colorimetric assay with phenolphthalein phosphate is clearly explained, linking assay principle to quantification of enzyme activity and supporting methodological choices.
The title is concise, accurate and immediately establishes the investigationâs focus, supporting a clear and professional structure in line with effective academic presentation.
There is occasional confusion between absorbance and inhibition magnitude; clarify that lower absorbance indicates greater inhibition to avoid conceptual ambiguity.
The description of uncompetitive inhibition for LiCl is accurate and demonstrates a strong grasp of enzyme kinetics, effectively linking mechanism to experimental design.

The research question is clearly stated but encompasses four inhibitors at once, stretching scope and adding uncontrolled variables; consider narrowing the focus to two inhibitors or justifying the choice more rigorously.
Including a precise word count at the start meets IB formatting requirements and demonstrates attention to administrative conventions.
The table of contents is comprehensive and accurately reflects the structure of the report, aiding navigation and reinforcing a clear organizational framework.
While the method is outlined logically, the choice of 0.1 M inhibitor concentration lacks explicit justification; include rationale for concentration to strengthen methodological rigor.
The chemical equation for phosphatase action is correctly depicted and clarifies the reaction mechanism, showing accurate use of biochemical notation and enhancing conceptual understanding.
The introduction provides coherent background on pharmacokinetics and enzymology, effectively situating phosphatase inhibition within biomedical relevance for a reader unfamiliar with the topic.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., redundant âonlineâ tags and varied punctuation); apply a single referencing style consistently throughout the bibliography.
Including means and standard deviations in preliminary tables demonstrates solid descriptive analysis, setting a strong foundation for inferential statistics.
The identification of precipitation as a confounding factor shows excellent critical evaluation and leads to actionable improvements in experimental design.
The p-value is correctly compared to the significance threshold (<0.01) to justify rejection of the null hypothesis, reflecting proper inferential reasoning.
Proposing a secondary filtration step to remove precipitates before absorbance measurement is an insightful methodological improvement to isolate true enzyme activity signals.
The overview of mixed inhibition by ZnSOâ is well articulated, showing correct application of terminology and a nuanced understanding of enzymeâinhibitor interactions.
The inhibition curves lack an explicit legend or description of error bars; add this information to ensure the reader can interpret variability and confidence in your data.
Only three replicates were performed for each condition; increasing to at least five replicates would improve statistical power and reliability of inferential tests.
The presentation of preliminary data uses mixed number formats (e.g., scientific notation for one standard deviation but decimals for others); unify formatting to enhance clarity and professionalism.
Uncertainties are provided for measuring cylinders but omitted for pipettes; include error margins for all volumetric glassware to fully justify uncertainty calculations.
Selecting a one-way ANOVA to compare multiple inhibitor groups is appropriate and demonstrates sound statistical decision-making aligned to the research question.

The ANOVA output is presented comprehensively, but the report does not explicitly state the critical F-value or degrees of freedom used to decide on significance; include these details to complete the inferential interpretation.
